aardvark: noun. The nocturnal, insectivorous, burrowing mammal Orycteropus afer, of the order Tubulidentata and somewhat resembling a pig.
arak: noun. A clear, unsweetened aniseed -flavoured alcoholic drink, produced and consumed primarily in the Levant.
ark: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikapú.
ava: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avar.
dak: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Dakota.
dark: adjective. Having an absolute or (more often) relative lack of light.
kava: noun. A plant from the South Pacific, Piper methysticum.
rad: symbol. radian, unit of measure for angle
radar: noun. In full primary radar: a method of detecting a distant object and determining its position, velocity, or other characteristics by
var: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huarijio.
vara: noun. A traditional Spanish unit of length, equivalent to about 83.7 cm.
ara: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Arabic.
arara: noun. The palm cockatoo, native to Australia and the Malay Archipelago (Probosciger aterrimus).
ard: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arabana.
dar: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Dargwa.
darr: noun. A bird, the black tern.
kara: noun. A bangle worn by Sikhs, one of the five Ks, to remind them to do God's work.
kra: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumhali.
kva: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bagvalal.
raad: noun. An assembly in one of the Boer republics of South Africa.
rada: noun. A parliamentary body in a number of Slavic countries.
vada: noun. A type of savoury doughnut eaten as a snack in south Asia.
